message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Viral infections have a history of abrupt and severe eruptions through the years in the form of pandemics. And yet, definitive therapies or preventive measures are not present.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Purpose</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Herbal medicines have been a source of various antiviral compounds. An accelerated repurposing potential of antiviral herbs can provide usable drugs and identify druggable targets. In this study, we dissect the anti-coronavirus activity of
                    <jats:italic>Cissampelos pareira</jats:italic>
                    L (
                    <jats:italic>Cipa</jats:italic>
                    ). using an integrative approach.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    We analysed the signature similarities between predicted antiviral agents and
                    <jats:italic>Cipa</jats:italic>
                    using the connectivity map (
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://clue.io/">https://clue.io/</jats:ext-link>
                    ). Next, we tested the anti-SARS-COV-2 activity of
                    <jats:italic>Cipa in vitro</jats:italic>
                    . A three-way comparative analysis of
                    <jats:italic>Cipa</jats:italic>
                    transcriptome, COVID-19 BALF transcriptome and CMAP signatures of small compounds was also performed.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Several predicted antivirals showed a high positive connectivity score with
                    <jats:italic>Cipa</jats:italic>
                    such as apcidin, emetine, homoharringtonine etc. We also observed 98% inhibition of SARS-COV-2 replication in infected Vero cell cultures with the whole extract. Some of its prominent pure constituents e.g pareirarine, cissamine, magnoflorine exhibited 40-80% inhibition. Comparison of genes between BALF and
                    <jats:italic>Cipa</jats:italic>
                    showed an enrichment of biological processes like transcription regulation and response to lipids, to be downregulated in
                    <jats:italic>Cipa</jats:italic>
                    while being upregulated in COVID-19. CMAP also showed that Triciribine, torin-1 and VU-0365114-2 had positive connectivity with BALF 1 and 2, and negative connectivity with
                    <jats:italic>Cipa</jats:italic>
                    .
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Anti-SARS-CoV-2 potential of Cissampelos pareira L. identified by Connectivity map-based analysis and in vitro studies

reference.unstructured: D. E. Gordon , et al., A SARS-CoV-2 protein interaction map reveals targets for drug repurposing. Nature (2020) https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-020-2286-9.
